title: SDD Micro-Spec
description: Canonical schema for Spec-Driven Development .spec.yaml files. This is the type definition for the type system.

id.description: Unique identifier. Lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens only. Used in depends_on references and test annotations.

version.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional pre-release tag).

status.description: Lifecycle status. Only 'approved' specs are enforced by spec-sync.

tier.description: Risk tier. 1 = Security/Money (strictest). 2 = Core Business Logic. 3 = Utility/Internal.

coverage_threshold.description: Per-spec coverage threshold override (0-100). Overrides the tier-based default for this spec only.

system.description: What system or service does this spec belong to?

feature.description: What feature area within the system?

description.description: Plain-language overview of why this spec exists.

existing_patterns.description: Relevant coding patterns, conventions, or architectural decisions.

related_specs.description: Other spec files that interact with this one.

assumptions.description: Things taken as given. If wrong, spec needs revision.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence description. Uses the Delta Principle: describes the change, not the state.

includes.description: Explicit list of what is in scope.

excludes.description: Explicit list of what is out of scope. Prevents AI scope creep.

constraints.description: Inviolable rules. Each is a hard boundary on the solution space.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable conditions that define 'done'. Each AC maps to at least one test.

depends_on.description: Other specs this depends on. Creates edges in the dependency graph.

tags.description: Free-form tags for categorization and filtering.

changelog.description: Version history. Most recent entry first.

source_file.description: Path to the source code this spec was reverse-compiled from.

generated_from.description: Present only on reverse-compiled specs. Tracks provenance.

id.description: Constraint ID. Format: C-01, C-02, etc.

description.description: Human-readable constraint. Should use RFC 2119 language (MUST, MUST NOT, SHOULD, MAY).

type.description: Category of constraint. Surfaces in spec-check diagnostics so issues can be grouped by category.

enforcement.description: Overrides the tier-based default severity when spec-check emits a diagnostic about this constraint. Omit to use the tier default.

validation.description: Optional machine-readable validation rule. Enables deterministic checking by spec-check.

field.description: The field this validation applies to.

value.description: The value for the rule. Type depends on the rule type.

id.description: Acceptance criterion ID. Format: AC-01, AC-02, etc.

description.description: Human-readable description of the expected behavior.

inputs.description: Input values or conditions that trigger this behavior.

expected_output.description: What should happen when the inputs are provided.

error_cases.description: Error conditions and their expected handling.

references_constraints.description: Which constraints this AC validates. Used for orphan detection.

gap.description: True if this AC was identified by gap analysis (no existing test covers it).

notes.description: Free-form narrative attached to this AC. Use for context, caveats, or cross-references to other ACs. Prefer the top-level changelog for version-over-version history; use notes for lifetime-of-the-AC annotation.

approval_gate.description: True when this AC requires explicit human approval before it can be considered done. Specter does not enforce approval semantics — teams wire this into their own PR/CI gates. See approval_date for when the approval was granted.

approval_date.description: Date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this AC was approved by a human reviewer. Meaningful only in conjunction with approval_gate: true.

spec_id.description: The id of the spec being depended on.

version_range.description: Semver range (e.g., '^1.0.0', '>=2.0.0 <3.0.0'). If omitted, any version accepted.
